data.body:
WS11 added to epic cite-stone-sea: removal-rationale criterion (Chesterton's Fence) -- net-new gap (NOT from DSO report), the removal-side DUAL of A1. Design settled in-session (no separate brainstorm):
- Two BRIGHT-LINE triggers (disjunction, no 'is it incidental' judgment): (1) removes/weakens externally-observable behavior/contract on ANY path incl. failure/timeout/exception semantics -- 'internal' defined by observable-behavior PRESERVATION not file locality, so an internals change to error/failure handling FIRES; (2) removes a check/test/validation guarding such behavior; (3) removes an artifact with an explicit intent marker (comment / #do-not-remove / referenced bug / bug-named test).
- EXEMPT: dead code, behavior-preserving refactor, mechanical/config/doc -- never nag legit simplification.
- PASS demonstration = a concrete TRIGGERING SCENARIO grounded in evidence (comment/test/blame/ticket), NOT invented, + why the reason no longer applies. Posture = constructive ('show we understand'), not adversarial ('poke holes'); reuses coach move 6 (spec-by-example). Grounding requirement blocks fabricated-scenario false-PASS.
- Checklist sub-answers are CRITERION-LOCAL (removes_external_behavior_or_guarded_fence + removal_scenario_grounded), NOT generic Pass-2 GRADED_BINARY additions -> genericity preserved.
- Advisory posture; test-removal in scope with E5.changed_behavior_tests overlap handled by Pass-4 coaching-pass grouping.
- Accepted limitation logged: a purely-latent guard (behavior-change only for never-exercised inputs, no intent marker) will not fire -- indistinguishable from dead code without external signal.
Ticket: pot-augur-ogle (f5d7-b2f2-1d70-4156).
